title: Portable Water Quality Monitor
description: Handheld Arduino-based water quality meter measuring pH, turbidity (NTU), and TDS (total dissolved solids) from a submersible probe assembly. Readings displayed on OLED; stored to microSD with timestamp; battery-powered with USB-C charging.
objective: Measure pH ±0.1, turbidity 0–1000 NTU ±5 %, TDS 0–2000 ppm ±5 %; store 1000 readings on SD; battery life ≥ 8 hours continuous use.
constraints: 3.7 V LiPo 1000 mAh with TP4056 charging; 3D-printed ABS enclosure IP54; ≤ £40 BOM; single Arduino Nano 3.3 V or 5 V with voltage divider for 3.3 V probes.
supporting_fact_titles: Water Quality Multi-Parameter Sensing (pH, TDS, DO)
pH Electrode — Calibration and Millivolt Measurement
OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface

element_usage_notes.pH Probe BNC Amplifier Interface: DFRobot SEN0161 module; analog output on A0; 2-point calibration (pH 4.01 + pH 7.00 buffer) stored in EEPROM; temperature-compensated using DS18B20 probe tip temperature.
element_usage_notes.Turbidity NTU Optical Sensor Module: DFRobot SEN0189; analog output on A1; polynomial calibration curve; sensor dipped in sample jar; LED off between readings to prevent self-heating artefacts.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: 128×64; 3-line layout: pH / NTU / TDS; blinking * when reading; press encoder to save reading to SD.
element_usage_notes.MicroSD 1 Hz Time-Series Data Logger: CSV log: seq, timestamp, pH, NTU, TDS, temp_c; encoder press triggers manual save; auto-save every 60 s.
element_usage_notes.Rotary Encoder User-Interface Navigation: KY-040; rotate to select parameter to calibrate; press to confirm; hold 2 s to trigger calibration mode.
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Nano 5 V; A0 = pH, A1 = turbidity, A2 = TDS; I²C (A4/A5) for OLED; D10 = SD CS; D2/D4/D3 = encoder CLK/DT/SW.

element_interactions.data: Analog voltage on A0 (0–5 V representing pH 0–14)
element_interactions.description: MCU samples A0 32× and averages; converts to pH via calibration formula: pH = 7 + (2.5 − V_avg) / 0.18. EEPROM-stored calibration offsets applied.

element_interactions.data: Analog voltage on A1 (0–4.5 V representing 0–3000 NTU)
element_interactions.description: MCU samples A1 16×, averages, then applies polynomial: NTU = −1120.4×V² + 5742.3×V − 4353.8. Values clamped to 0–3000 NTU.

element_interactions.data: Direction delta (±1) and press events via GPIO interrupts
element_interactions.description: Short press triggers manual SD save of current readings with timestamp; hold 2 s enters calibration menu for pH two-point procedure.

element_interactions.data: I²C framebuffer (ph, ntu, tds, temp_c, save_indicator)
element_interactions.description: OLED updated at 1 Hz. pH displayed as XX.X with ±0.1 resolution; NTU as integer; TDS in ppm. Blinks save icon after SD write.

element_interactions.data: CSV row (seq, elapsed_s, pH, NTU, TDS, temp_c)
element_interactions.description: Auto-save every 60 s plus manual save on encoder press. Filename WQLOG.CSV; appends to existing file on power-cycle.
